title: VASTE
subtitle: Runtime R&D
abstract: VASTE is the future Electronic Artefacts runtime platform, currently in R&D and targeted for delivery in 2027.
description: VASTE connects Runtime Theory to an in-development runtime for graph systems, contextual execution, identity and modular world construction.
mandate: Develop a reusable runtime platform for contextual execution, identity, knowledge structures, simulation and modular systems, with delivery targeted for 2027.
bodyHtml:
Mandate

VASTE is the future runtime platform developed within Electronic Artefacts. It is currently in R&D, where it explores graph architectures, contextual execution, identity systems, simulation frameworks, knowledge structures and modular world construction. Its intended delivery horizon is 2027.

VASTE is not currently a public product, a download or a commercial service. The public record describes the direction of the research and the architectural questions being developed; availability, interfaces and access conditions will be announced only when the platform is ready.

Research foundation

The program operationalizes Runtime Theory. It treats entities, relations, identity and context as executable system concerns rather than only database or interface concerns.

Architecture

The current architecture is organized around addressable graph entities, typed relationships, contextual identity and event propagation. Research prototypes can build specialized surfaces while retaining shared runtime semantics.

The program is structured around several operating layers:

  • Graph entity layer: canonical records, typed references, routeable identifiers and semantic publication.
  • Relationship layer: explicit predicates, bidirectional context and local graph views.
  • Identity and permission layer: boundaries for users, organizations, maintainers, access and visibility.
  • Event execution layer: state changes, propagation and system responses.
  • Projection layer: public pages, product surfaces, search documents, feeds and external interfaces.

Lineage

VASTE follows ARCA, an unpublished earlier system with a substantial codebase. ARCA explored digital assets, traceability and certification but was never released. Its scope later moved toward generalized execution and information structures.

Research applications

V6 is a future platform intended as an applied test context for VASTE. It will map art and craft practices and develop a CRM-oriented layer for that ecosystem. Domains and social presences are reserved, but the platform remains in development with the same 2027 horizon.

Other Electronic Artefacts work may inform or test parts of the research, but none should be presented as a released VASTE product before delivery.

Status and limitations

VASTE remains in active R&D. Architectural claims are published only where they are stable enough to explain; implementation details, performance characteristics, release timing and product scope remain subject to revision. The present target is delivery in 2027.
